industry.summary: A.P. Moller Capital, a global institutional fund manager, has signed an agreement to acquire 100% of Mainstream Renewable Power South Africa. The acquisition strengthens A.P. Moller's presence in African renewable energy markets. Mainstream SA develops and operates wind and solar projects across South Africa.

industry.headline: Bangladesh Tenders 95 MW Solar Projects
industry.summary: The Bangladesh Power Development Board (BPDB) has issued a tender to install 95 MW of grid-connected solar projects across Rangamati and Cox’s Bazar. The initiative aims to expand the country’s renewable energy capacity. Interested developers can bid for the solar projects under the government’s renewable energy policy.

tech.headline: Waterproofing and Load Testing Critical for Solar Mounting on Metal Roofs

tech.headline: German Researchers Set 25.5% Efficiency Record for Tandem Solar Cell
tech.summary: German researchers achieved a world record 25.5% efficiency for a perovskite-CIGS tandem solar cell, confirmed by the European Solar Test Installation (ESTI). The breakthrough, reported by PV Magazine, highlights advancements in photovoltaic technology. The result underscores ongoing innovation in solar cell performance optimization.

tech.headline: Vertical Agrivoltaics Improve Oat Crop Performance in Sweden Study
tech.summary: Swedish researchers found oat production under vertical agrivoltaic systems outperformed ground-mounted and open-field conditions during the 2024 growing season. The study, published in PV Magazine, demonstrates dual land use potential. Results suggest agrivoltaics enhance crop performance while generating solar energy.

tech.headline: Maxeon and Qcells Resolve Global Patent Dispute Over TOPCon Technology
tech.summary: Maxeon Solar Technologies and Qcells settled their global patent dispute, ending litigation over TOPCon solar cell technology. The agreement, reported by PV Magazine, resolves claims related to intellectual property. Both companies will now focus on commercializing the technology without legal conflicts.

learn.title: Production Linked Incentive for solar manufacturing in India
learn.intro: Solar energy transition demands innovative incentives to boost manufacturing efficiency and reduce reliance on fossil fuels. India's push for domestic solar production hinges on aligning policy with global sustainability goals, ensuring affordable, scalable solutions while addressing regional economic challenges.
learn.c1_text: Government subsidies and tax breaks are critical to scaling up solar panel production, directly impacting job creation and reducing carbon footprints.
learn.c2_text: These measures encourage private sector investment by aligning financial benefits with renewable energy goals, accelerating grid integration and long-term decarbonization.
learn.c3_text: India estimates 90 GW of solar potential by 2030, necessitating targeted policies to meet this target while balancing infrastructure and environmental impacts.

learn.title: Perovskite solar cells — next generation efficiency beyond silicon
learn.intro: Perovskite solar cells promise higher efficiencies and lower costs compared to traditional silicon, offering a promising pathway to democratize renewable energy access globally.
learn.c1_text: With efficiencies exceeding 30%, these materials could revolutionize solar technology, potentially cutting costs by 50% while enhancing performance in diverse climates.
learn.c2_text: Their combination of high efficiency and adaptability positions them as a cornerstone for future energy systems requiring widespread adoption.
learn.c3_text: Perovskite research accelerates renewable energy adoption, addressing critical gaps in current solar technology while maintaining environmental benefits.

learn.title: Renewable Purchase Obligation in India — what it means for DISCOMs
learn.intro: DISCOMs face regulatory pressures to increase renewable procurement, reshaping India's energy landscape and challenging traditional utility models.
learn.c1_text: Mandating DISCOMs to source 50% renewables by 2030 drives investment in solar and wind, though balancing affordability with infrastructure demands remains a challenge.
learn.c2_text: Compliance requires significant grid upgrades and financing support, testing the resilience of existing energy systems in rural areas.
learn.c3_text: India's policy shift aims to curb emissions while ensuring equitable access, requiring careful stakeholder coordination.

learn.title: HVDC transmission lines — sending power across thousands of kilometres
learn.intro: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems are vital for transporting renewable energy over long distances, overcoming geographical and technical barriers efficiently.
learn.c1_text: These lines minimize losses and costs, enabling seamless integration of wind and solar power into national grids despite geographical constraints.
learn.c2_text: By transmitting power efficiently across continents, HVDC supports regional energy security and economic growth.
learn.c3_text: Critical for connecting remote areas to markets, HVDC infrastructure is pivotal for India's energy transition.

learn.title: Microgrids — energy independence for villages and disaster resilience
learn.intro: Microgrids provide localized energy solutions, enhancing resilience during natural disasters and reducing dependency on centralized grids.
learn.c1_text: These networks empower communities with reliable power, especially in regions prone to outages or political instability.
learn.c2_text: By integrating renewables and storage, microgrids ensure consistent energy access while promoting sustainability.
learn.c3_text: They act as backup systems, maintaining critical services during catastrophic events, thus safeguarding socio-economic stability.

learn.title: Hydrogen fuel cells — how they generate electricity cleanly in vehicles
learn.intro: Hydrogen fuel cells offer a clean alternative to fossil fuels, providing zero-emission power solutions for transportation and industry.
learn.c1_text: With no combustion byproducts, they enable scalable decarbonization across sectors requiring reliable, continuous power.
learn.c2_text: These devices convert hydrogen into electricity efficiently, supporting both stationary and mobile applications globally.
learn.c3_text: Hydrogen's role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate industries like manufacturing and heavy transport remains pivotal.
